text: Risk management is an important process for any business or project. It begins with identifying risks, such as financial loss, delays, or safety issues. Once risks are recognised, companies develop mitigation strategies to reduce their impact. For example, a construction company might buy insurance to protect against accidents. Even with good planning, unexpected problems can occur, so contingency plans are created. A contingency plan outlines specific steps to take if a risk becomes real, like using backup suppliers if one fails. Finally, monitoring is essential. Teams must regularly review risks and update plans as situations change. This continuous cycle helps organisations stay prepared and avoid major losses.
